title: Calculus
excerpt: The gold standard for a rigorous first course in calculus: proves everything, demands everything, and rewards everything. Not a computation book — a thinking book.
whoShouldRead: Anyone who wants to truly understand calculus, not just compute
Readers who enjoy elegant proofs and challenging problems
keyIdeas: Rigorous construction of real numbers via Dedekind cuts
epsilon-delta definition of limits proved from first principles
The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus proved rigorously
Sequences and series with full convergence theory
The topology of the real line
keyTakeaways: Calculus is not about formulas — it's about proving why they work
The real numbers have a precise logical structure
Every theorem in calculus has hypotheses that matter
Mathematical rigor is not pedantry — it's clarity
